basic_info.title: Legend of Mana
basic_info.review: A masterpiece of non-linear design with a stunning soundtrack and unique world-building mechanics that set it apart from traditional RPGs.

alternative_names: 聖剣伝説 レジェンド・オブ・マナ

play_today.details: The definitive modern experience via Steam. Features native widescreen support, controller remapping, and cloud saves. Includes the remaster's enhanced content like the new dungeon and voice acting. Mod support allows UI overhauls and QoL improvements not present in original. Best performance with 60 FPS and 4K scaling options.

recommended_patches.name: Widescreen Fix for Legend of Mana
recommended_patches.description: Enables native support for ultra-wide and custom resolutions on the Windows HD Remaster, fixing UI stretching and black bars on non-16:9 monitors.

recommended_patches.name: Legend of Mana HD Remaster Mod
recommended_patches.description: Community mod for the Windows version adding UI scaling improvements, faster menu navigation, and quality of life enhancements not present in the base game.

recommended_patches.name: ReShade Preset for Legend of Mana
recommended_patches.description: Enhances visual fidelity on Windows by applying color correction and sharpening filters to the remastered graphics, improving clarity on 4K displays.

play_today.details: Best portable option with full remaster content including new dungeon and voice acting. Runs at 30 FPS in handheld mode with occasional frame drops, but offers the convenience of playing anywhere. Graphics are slightly scaled down compared to PS4/PC but still look sharp. Ideal for commuters or those preferring handheld gaming.

For purists seeking the authentic 1999 experience, the original PlayStation version is the definitive choice. While it lacks HD graphics and widescreen support, it retains the original aspect ratio and the specific feel of the original hardware. It is highly recommended to use an unofficial English translation patch to access the full narrative without language barriers.

recommended_patches.name: Legend of Mana English Translation Patch
recommended_patches.description: An unofficial fan translation patch that converts the Japanese text of the original PlayStation version into English. It includes all dialogue, menu options, item descriptions, and cutscene text, allowing players to experience the game in its original form with full narrative comprehension.

play_today.details: Excellent console experience with native 1080p resolution and stable 60 FPS. Includes all remaster features like the new dungeon and voice acting. Convenient for TV play with controller support and trophy integration. Slightly less flexible than PC regarding mods and resolution scaling but offers a polished, plug-and-play experience.

description.synopsis: The game follows a young girl named Riesz who dreams of a mystical Mana Tree and a world in chaos. Upon waking, she discovers her dream is a prophecy and sets out to find the tree before the world is consumed by darkness. Along the way, she encounters a diverse cast of characters, each with their own motivations and stories, all tied to the fate of the Mana Tree.
key_features: Land Make System: Non-linear world map creation where players place towns and dungeons to shape the story
Real-time Action Combat: Fast-paced swordplay with no turn-based waiting
Dynamic Weapon System: Weapons have unique stats and can be upgraded or fused
Non-Linear Story Progression: Multiple quest paths and endings based on player choices
Unique Character Classes: Distinct classes like the 'Dancer' and 'Monk' with specific mechanics
Orchestral Soundtrack: Composed by Yoko Shimomura, featuring a distinct fantasy style
Dream World Mechanic: The game begins in a dream state that influences the waking world
Mana Tree Lore: Central plot device connecting all characters and events
legacy_and_impact: Legend of Mana is widely regarded as one of the most unique entries in the Mana series and the broader RPG genre due to its revolutionary 'Land Make' system. It established a cult following for its non-linear storytelling and Yoko Shimomura's iconic soundtrack, influencing later games that prioritize player agency in world-building.
